data.title: ASTEROID Creator's Mother Withdraws Funds Amid Threats
data.description: The mother of ASTEROID's creator reported that she withdrew and transferred funds related to Bags on the same day due to threats. According to NS3.AI, she claimed her social media account was compromised and stated that she has not conducted any further operations since the incident.

data.title: DWF Labs Partner Highlights Challenges in DeFi Market Yields
data.description: Andrei Grachev, a partner at DWF Labs, has expressed concerns about the sustainability of high yields in the current decentralized finance (DeFi) market. According to NS3.AI, Grachev noted that these yields are often reliant on mechanisms such as airdrops, recursive lending, and incentive subsidies. He emphasized that these factors contribute to the difficulty in maintaining truly risk-free returns over an extended period.

data.title: Upbit will launch CHIP Korean Won pair
data.description: According to an official announcement, Upbit will launch CHIP (Korean Won) pairs.

data.title: UK Wage Growth Exceeds Expectations in February
data.description: The UK's average wage growth excluding bonuses for the three months ending in February was reported at 3.6%, surpassing the anticipated 3.5% and slightly below the previous figure of 3.8%. According to Jin10, this data indicates a steady increase in wages despite economic uncertainties. The wage growth reflects ongoing labor market dynamics and could influence future monetary policy decisions by the Bank of England.

data.title: DWF Labs Partner: DeFi yields are not risk-free, and high returns are unsustainable in the long run.
data.description: Andrei Grachev, a partner at DWF Labs, stated that the current state of the DeFi market indicates that truly risk-free returns do not exist. While DeFi has value in improving transaction efficiency, high returns typically rely on factors such as airdrops, revolving lending, and incentive subsidies, making them difficult to sustain in the long term.

data.title: Binance Shares Tips for Successful Live Streaming
data.description:
Binance announced on X a set of strategies aimed at enhancing live streaming experiences for both novice and experienced users. The guidance focuses on establishing a strong personal brand, maintaining a steady pace, minimizing awkward pauses, and increasing potential earnings during live broadcasts. These tips are shared as part of Binance's 'Live Streaming Carnival Season,' encouraging users to apply the strategies immediately to improve their streaming sessions.

The initiative is designed to help new streamers get started while offering seasoned broadcasters ways to refine their skills. By emphasizing the importance of creating a relatable persona, streamers can engage more effectively with their audience. Maintaining a consistent rhythm throughout the broadcast helps keep viewers interested and reduces the likelihood of losing engagement due to prolonged silences. Additionally, the advice includes methods to optimize revenue generation during live streams, providing practical steps for users to enhance their financial outcomes.

Binance's announcement underscores the platform's commitment to supporting its community by offering valuable insights and tools for content creators. The 'Live Streaming Carnival Season' serves as an opportunity for users to implement these strategies and potentially boost their streaming success. This initiative reflects Binance's broader efforts to foster a dynamic and interactive environment where users can thrive and maximize their potential in the digital space.

data.title: Upcoming Release of Key Economic Indicators from Switzerland and the UK
data.description: Switzerland's March trade balance, along with the UK's February three-month ILO unemployment rate, March jobless claims, and unemployment rate, are set to be released in ten minutes. According to Jin10, these indicators are closely watched by market participants for insights into economic conditions and labor market trends in both countries.

data.title: SpaceX Executive Compensation Revealed in Recent Filing
data.description: SpaceX's recent filing has disclosed significant executive compensation figures. According to BlockBeats, the document reveals that President Gwynne Shotwell received a total compensation of $85.8 million last year, placing her among the highest-paid executives in the United States. Shotwell, who also serves as Chief Operating Officer, has a base salary of $1 million, with the majority of her compensation coming from stock options and bonuses. The filing also highlights that Chief Financial Officer Bret Johnson earned a total of $9.8 million. Meanwhile, CEO Elon Musk, a major shareholder in SpaceX, paid himself a salary of only $54,080.

data.title: Who Is Building Homes and Setting Roles for AI Agents?
data.description: According to PANews, the development of AI agents involves various stakeholders who are responsible for creating environments, providing resources, and defining roles. These agents are designed to perform specific tasks and require a structured framework to operate effectively. The process includes setting up virtual spaces where AI agents can function, as well as establishing guidelines for their interactions and responsibilities. The collaboration among developers, engineers, and policymakers is crucial in shaping the future of AI agents, ensuring they are integrated into society in a beneficial and ethical manner. As AI technology continues to evolve, the roles and responsibilities of these agents are expected to expand, necessitating ongoing oversight and adaptation.

data.title: SpaceX's prospectus disclosed executive compensation, with Musk receiving a symbolic salary.
data.description: SpaceX's prospectus reveals that President Gwynne Shotwell's total compensation last year reached $85.8 million, placing her among the highest-paid executives in the United States. Shotwell, who also serves as Chief Operating Officer, has a base salary of $1 million, with the majority of her compensation coming from stock options and awards. The prospectus summary shows that Chief Financial Officer Bret Johnson's total compensation was $9.8 million; while CEO Elon Musk, a major shareholder in SpaceX, paid only $54,080 for himself. (Jinshi)

data.description:
1. ASTEROID author's mother: Social media accounts hacked; she did not publish any crypto-related content.
2. Yi Lihua: There may be another major correction, which is also an excellent last-minute buying opportunity.
3. Tom Lee: Retail investors will return to the market and become the main driving force for this round of gains.
4. ASTEROID's market capitalization surpasses a new high of $200 million, up over 48% intraday.
5. SpaceX holds closed-door talks with Wall Street before its IPO, planning to reserve approximately 30% of its shares for retail investors.
6. Dune: Nearly half of LayerZero OApps have used a 1-of-1 DVN configuration in the past 90 days.
7. Vercel releases security incident analysis: Third-party AI tools were compromised, leading to unauthorized access; no sensitive data tampering has been reported.
8. Bezos' AI LAB is valued at nearly $38 billion in its funding round.
9. Pharos releases PROS token economic model: initial supply of 1 billion tokens, zero inflation for the first 6 months.

data.title: BASED briefly broke through $0.16, with a daily increase of 56%.
data.description: Market data shows that, possibly influenced by the news that Bithumb will launch the BASED Korean Won trading pair, BASED briefly touched 0.16 USDT and is currently trading at 0.15 USDT, representing a daily increase of 56%.

data.title: Russell 2000's April Surge Sparks Altcoin Season Speculation
data.description: The Russell 2000 index experienced a significant increase of 11.8% in April, reaching a record high of 2,798. According to NS3.AI, this development has reignited discussions about the potential for an altcoin season. Tony Severino noted that the correlation between the Russell 2000 and altcoins has turned negative for the first time since July 2016, which may weaken the historical signal that traders have relied upon.

data.title: Bithumb will launch BASED
data.description: According to an official announcement, South Korean cryptocurrency exchange Bithumb will list BASED on the Korean won market.

data.title: Coin Center: Code should be protected by the First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, and developers should not be held responsible for the results of its use.
data.description: Coin Center released a report stating that cryptographic software code constitutes "functional speech" and should be protected by the First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution. The organization argues that writing and releasing code is similar to writing a book or releasing a recipe, with developers acting as "expressors and inventors," not asset custodians or intermediaries. The report points out that simply releasing and maintaining software should be strictly protected, but when developers directly control user assets, execute transactions on behalf of users, or make decisions on their behalf, they may fall under regulatory oversight. This statement comes amid escalating regulatory controversy. Coin Center emphasizes that developers should not be treated as financial intermediaries due to ease of law enforcement, and calls for the continuation of existing principles of free speech in the context of new technologies, rather than expanding the boundaries of criminal liability. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Coin Center Advocates for First Amendment Protection of Crypto Software Code
data.description:
Coin Center has released a report asserting that cryptocurrency software code should be considered 'functional speech' and protected under the First Amendment of the U.S. Constitution. According to Odaily, the organization argues that writing and publishing code is akin to authoring a book or sharing a recipe, positioning developers as 'expressers and inventors' rather than custodians or intermediaries.

The report highlights that the mere act of publishing and maintaining software should be strictly safeguarded. However, when developers directly control user assets, execute transactions, or make decisions on behalf of users, they may fall within a regulatory scope.

This statement comes amid increasing regulatory disputes. Coin Center emphasizes that developers should not be treated as financial intermediaries for the sake of enforcement convenience. The organization calls for the continuation of existing free speech principles in the context of new technologies, rather than expanding the boundaries of criminal liability.

data.title: Binance Margin to Delist Multiple Trading Pairs in April 2026
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform's Margin service is set to delist several margin trading pairs on April 24, 2026, at 06:00 (UTC). The affected pairs include both cross and isolated margin pairs. Cross Margin Pairs to be delisted are AAVE/ETH, STX/BTC, ICP/BTC, SEI/BTC, AAVE/BTC, UNI/BTC, LTC/ETH, NEAR/BTC, XLM/BTC, and ADA/ETH. The Isolated Margin Pairs include STX/BTC, ICP/BTC, SEI/BTC, AAVE/BTC, UNI/BTC, LTC/ETH, NEAR/BTC, XLM/BTC, and ADA/ETH.

Effective immediately, users are unable to transfer assets of these pairs into their Isolated Margin accounts via manual transfers or Auto-Transfer Mode. Users with outstanding liabilities of these tokens can only manually transfer up to the amount of liabilities into their Isolated Margin accounts, excluding any collateral already available. On April 22, 2026, at 06:00 (UTC), Binance Margin will suspend isolated margin borrowing for these pairs. Subsequently, on April 24, 2026, at 06:00 (UTC), Binance Margin will close users’ positions, conduct automatic settlements, and cancel all pending orders on the affected cross and isolated margin pairs. These pairs will then be removed from Binance Margin.

Users are advised to close their positions and transfer assets from Margin Accounts to Spot Accounts before the cessation of Margin trading on April 24, 2026, at 06:00 (UTC). Binance emphasizes that it will not be responsible for any potential losses incurred during this process. The delisting process may take approximately three hours, during which users will not be able to update their positions. Despite the delisting, users can continue trading the affected assets on other available trading pairs within Binance Margin.

data.title: Justin Sun Discusses AI Financial Infrastructure at Hong Kong Web3 Carnival
data.description: During the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, Justin Sun highlighted challenges in adapting existing payment systems to the development of AI agents. According to Foresight News, Sun announced that B.AI is building financial infrastructure tailored for the AI era, with plans to launch a 'Clone Brain' feature next week. B.AI offers automated on-chain payment settlements and privacy protection, along with dedicated identity and historical record features.

data.title: Binance Leverage will remove some trading pairs, such as AAVE/ETH.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance Leverage will remove the following leveraged trading pairs on April 24, 2026 at 14:00 (UTC+8): Cross Margin Trading Pairs: AAVE/ETH, STX/BTC, ICP/BTC, SEI/BTC, AAVE/BTC, UNI/BTC, LTC/ETH, NEAR/BTC, XLM/BTC, ADA/ETH; Isolated Margin Trading Pairs: STX/BTC, ICP/BTC, SEI/BTC, AAVE/BTC, UNI/BTC, LTC/ETH, NEAR/BTC, XLM/BTC, ADA/ETH.

data.title: JPMorgan Raises S&P 500 Target for 2026
data.description: JPMorgan has revised its target for the S&P 500 index by the end of 2026, increasing it to 7,600 points from the previous target of 7,200 points. According to ChainCatcher, this adjustment reflects the bank's updated outlook on the market's performance over the next few years.
